1. **Function $ L(t) $ for LED Light Output:**

We are given the natural light function:
$$
N(t) = 300 + 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)
$$
and the desired total illumination is 500 lumens. The LED light output $ L(t) $ must be such that the total illumination is 500 lumens. This means:
$$
N(t) + L(t) = 500
$$
Solving for $ L(t) $:
$$
L(t) = 500 - N(t)
$$
$$
L(t) = 500 - \left(300 + 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)\right)
$$
$$
L(t) = 200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)
$$

This function ensures that the total illumination remains constant at 500 lumens throughout the day.

**Energy Consumption Over 24 Hours:**

The power consumption of the LEDs is 0.1 watts per lumen-hour. The total energy consumption is:
$$
\text{Total Energy} = \int_0^{24} L(t) \cdot 0.1 \, dt
$$
Substitute $ L(t) $:
$$
\text{Total Energy} = \int_0^{24} \left(200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)\right) \cdot 0.1 \, dt
$$
$$
= 0.1 \int_0^{24} \left(200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)\right) \, dt
$$
$$
= 0.1 \left[ 200t + 200 \int_0^{24} \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right) dt \right]
$$
